title: "Reimagining Cultural Heritage in the Digital Age, A Journey Through Research"
subtitle: "Professorial Innagural"
author: "Professor Karina Rodriguez Echavarria"
institute: "University of Brighton"
date: "23 April, 2026"
slide-number : true
show-slide-number : print
footer: "yes"
output: powerpoint_presentation
#  powerpoint_presentation:
# reference_doc: pres_15Apr.pptx
---
  
  
  ::: {.notes}
As our lives become increasingly digital, so too does our experience of culture. Advances in computing are transforming artefacts, historic environments, and cultural practices into interactive digital experiences. These "digital twins" do more than preserve the past — they make heritage accessible beyond physical museums and archives, opening up new possibilities for creativity, education, and public engagement.

This talk traces the motivations, questions and continuous evolution of my research,
and the wider research landscape on digital innovation.
From early work on systems to share information stemeed from the
availability of early web technologies,
to the current advances of Artificial Intelligence or AI
for digital cultural heritage, my story 
reveals how technology has progressively transformed 
not just *how* we document and preserve culture, 
but *who* can access it, 
*how* we engage with it, 
and *what* questions we must ask about ownership, 
representation, and sustainability.

I would also like to explore technologies ability to 
help humans discover/better understand humanity - past and present - through the 
material culture they create and their immaterial heritage.

Let's first look at my human-self

How technologies help us understand what defines as humans
How we make it work to keep this humanity forward going
::::

<!-- Slide 1 -->

<!-- # Introduction {background-image="images/im.jpg"} -->

<!-- ## {background-image="images/im.jpg"} -->


# Slide 1

Test [^1]


[^1]: This appears at the bottom of the current slide.
